sections.subtitle: Current Form Analysis
sections.text: Chelsea are in strong form, unbeaten in their last 8 matches, with impressive attacking performances in recent games, scoring multiple goals in 4 of their last 5. West Ham United, on the other hand, have struggled, losing 3-0 in their last league match and sitting at 19th in the standings with no points.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Chelsea have dominated recent head-to-head encounters, winning 3 of the last 5 matches against West Ham, including a convincing 3-0 victory in their most recent meeting. West Ham have failed to keep a clean sheet in their last 11 games against Chelsea.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Chelsea show superior attacking intent with higher expected goals (1.60 vs. West Ham’s 0.56 in recent games) and more big chances created in matches. West Ham’s defense has been leaky, conceding 3 goals in their only league game, while their attack has yet to score.

sections.subtitle: Key Players
sections.text: Cole Palmer has been pivotal for Chelsea, averaging a 7.54 Sofascore rating and contributing 2 goals in the last 5 matches. Jarrod Bowen is West Ham’s standout with 4 goals recently, but his impact may be limited against Chelsea’s strong defense.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, Chelsea lead West Ham United 3-1 at the London Stadium. Chelsea have dominated the game with superior ball possession and a clinical edge in attack, scoring three goals through João Pedro, Pedro Neto, and Enzo Fernández. West Ham managed to pull one back early through Lucas Paquetá, but they’ve struggled to create significant opportunities against a well-organized Chelsea side. The stats reflect Chelsea’s control, with more final third entries and a higher expected goals tally, while West Ham have been on the back foot for much of the half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea started the match with strong attacking intent, dictating the early momentum and pushing forward with purpose. West Ham had a brief spell of pressure around the time of their goal, showing some fight in the attacking third. However, Chelsea quickly regained control, with sustained periods of dominance leading to their three goals. There were moments where West Ham tried to claw back into the game with bursts of energy late in the half, but Chelsea’s consistent attacking presence overshadowed these efforts for the majority of the time.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Chelsea, João Pedro has been a standout, contributing with a goal and an assist, proving to be a constant threat. Pedro Neto and Enzo Fernández also made their mark with well-taken goals, showcasing Chelsea’s attacking depth. On the West Ham side, Lucas Paquetá has been the bright spot, scoring their only goal and showing creativity in midfield. Jarrod Bowen has tried to spark attacks, but lacks support, while defensively, Nayef Aguerd has been busy with clearances under pressure from Chelsea’s relentless forward play.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Chelsea have controlled the game with high possession and effective use of the final third, often exploiting spaces in West Ham’s defense with quick transitions. Their ability to win duels and aerial battles has further solidified their dominance. West Ham, on the other hand, have struggled to maintain possession and create big chances, often resorting to long balls or isolated attacks. Defensively, they’ve been vulnerable, with gaps in the backline that Chelsea have capitalized on repeatedly.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Looking ahead to the second half, Chelsea are likely to continue their high-pressing game and look for additional goals to secure the result. They may focus on maintaining possession to control the tempo and exploit any fatigue in West Ham’s defense. West Ham will need a tactical shift, possibly introducing fresh legs in midfield or attack to regain some momentum. They’ll aim to be more compact defensively and capitalize on any set-piece opportunities to get back into the contest. However, given Chelsea’s current form and confidence, it will be a tough ask for the hosts to turn this around.
